creator: nox@jelal.kn-bremen.de
text:
My _guess_ what happened is you installed some release packages
from the isos or via pkg_add -r, grabbed an up to date portstree
(which was newer than the one the release packages were built from)
and then ran "make install" in /usr/ports/x11/kde4.  That this
rarely works is a known problem:  "make install" doesn't upgrade
already installed ports/packages that the one you are trying to
install depends on, and it also doesn't run manual upgrade steps
listed in /usr/ports/UPDATING, causing the issues you saw.
See also the ports chapter in the handbook which documents
/usr/ports/UPDATING under Upgrading Ports:

	http://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/handbook/ports-using.html

(I'm sorry upgrading ports isn't as straightforward as you may be
used to from other operating systems...  We are working on it.)
